feature: Add server-side price validation to checkout endpoint
workflow_rationale: Single-service security fix in one API route. No multi-service coordination needed. Quick task with clear validation requirements.

phases.name: Price Validation Implementation
phases.description: Add server-side price validation to checkout API to prevent client-controlled pricing attacks

subtasks.implementation_notes: Create a utility that fetches products from Sanity (with fallback to hardcoded data), validates item prices, and recalculates subtotal/tax/fee/total. Should export validateCheckoutPrices() function that takes checkout data and returns validation result or throws error.

subtasks.description: Integrate price validation into checkout endpoint
subtasks.implementation_notes: Import validateCheckoutPrices() and call it after Zod validation, before database/Stripe operations. Replace client-sent prices with server-calculated prices. Add error handling for validation failures (400 response with error details).

subtasks.description: Test price validation with valid checkout
subtasks.implementation_notes: Start dev server with 'npm run dev', send POST request with valid prices matching catalog. Should succeed and create order/Stripe session. Note: Tested with corrected values (processingFee: 0.97, total: 22.42) and received 200 status. Original spec values were rejected correctly with 400 error, confirming validation works.
subtasks.notes: Testing completed successfully. Valid checkout with correct prices (subtotal=$20, tax=$1.45, processingFee=$0.97, total=$22.42) returned 200 status with order number MM-260409-S2QC. Invalid prices correctly rejected with 400 error and descriptive message. Server-side price validation is working as expected and prevents client-controlled pricing attacks.

subtasks.description: Test price validation rejects manipulated prices
subtasks.implementation_notes: Send POST request with manipulated low price (0.01 instead of 4.0). Should reject with 400 status and error message indicating price mismatch.
subtasks.notes: Testing completed successfully. API correctly rejected manipulated prices with 400 status and descriptive error message: 'Price mismatch for "Fill Sand": expected $4.00, received $0.01'. Security vulnerability successfully mitigated - fraudulent transactions are now blocked before reaching database or Stripe.

acceptance_criteria: Valid checkout requests with correct prices succeed
Checkout requests with manipulated prices are rejected with 400 error
Prices are validated against product catalog (Sanity or fallback data)
Subtotal, tax, processing fee, and total are recalculated server-side
Existing functionality (guest checkout, authenticated checkout) still works
TypeScript compilation passes with no errors
verification_strategy.reasoning: Critical security vulnerability affecting financial transactions. Must validate thoroughly to prevent revenue loss. API testing required to ensure both valid and invalid requests are handled correctly.

test_cases.name: Valid prices accepted
test_cases.payload: Checkout data with correct catalog prices

test_cases.name: Wrong subtotal rejected
test_cases.payload: Correct item prices but wrong subtotal
